meta.title: Proxmox Monitor API — Integration Reference | ProxMenux
meta.description: HTTP endpoints exposed by ProxMenux Monitor for integrations: Home Assistant, Homepage, Grafana, Prometheus, n8n and custom dashboards. Read-only data export plus the safe write operations needed by automations — VM control, backup trigger, notification dispatch, alert acknowledgement.
meta.ogDescription: Endpoints exposed by ProxMenux Monitor for Home Assistant, Homepage, Grafana, Prometheus, n8n and custom dashboards.
meta.twitterDescription: Endpoints for Home Assistant, Homepage, Grafana, Prometheus, n8n and custom dashboards.

header.title: API Reference
header.description: The HTTP endpoints integrators use to read state and trigger safe actions on ProxMenux Monitor — Home Assistant sensors, Homepage cards, Grafana dashboards, Prometheus scrapes, n8n flows and custom scripts. Every category, plus a complete list of Prometheus metrics, with curl examples.

intro.title: What this page is for
intro.body: This page lists the endpoints we expect external integrations to use — read-only data export across every part of the Monitor, plus the small set of write operations that automations legitimately need (trigger a backup, send a custom notification, acknowledge an alert). The whole API runs from the same Flask process that serves the dashboard UI on TCP 8008; bind address and TLS are configured in Access & Authentication.

auth.heading: Authentication
auth.intro: Every endpoint marked "authenticated" expects a JWT bearer token in the request header:
auth.tokensIntro: Two ways to obtain the token:
items: API tokens (recommended for integrations). Long-lived tokens minted from Settings → Security → API Tokens in the dashboard. Each token is named, can be revoked individually, and is what you should hand to Home Assistant / Homepage / Grafana / n8n.

Login flow (short-lived JWT). POST /api/auth/login with a username, password and TOTP token if 2FA is enabled. The returned JWT is short-lived and refreshed by the dashboard automatically; useful for ad-hoc scripts that authenticate as a human user.
auth.flowLink: The auth flow, password policy, 2FA setup, audit log and TLS configuration all live in <link>Access & Authentication</link>.

rows.use: Body: '{'"username","password","totp_token?"'}'. Returns a short-lived JWT.
List API tokens (metadata only — names, prefixes, dates; never the actual secret).
Mint a new long-lived API token. Body: '{'"name":"'<'label'>'"'}'. The token value is returned once and cannot be retrieved again.
Revoke a specific API token by its ID.

conventions.heading: Conventions
items: All requests and responses are JSON unless explicitly noted (log download is plain text, /api/prometheus is text/plain in the OpenMetrics format, task log is plain text).
Successful mutating endpoints return '{'"success": true, ...'}'. Error responses use a non-2xx HTTP status with '{'"success": false, "error": "'<'reason'>'"'}'.
List endpoints accept optional limit, offset, since and category-specific filters via query string.
Time fields are returned as Unix epoch seconds or ISO-8601 with explicit timezone, never as locale strings.

rows.use: CPU, memory, swap, uptime, load — current snapshot.
Static host info: hostname, kernel, PVE version, CPU model, distro.
Extended system snapshot used by the dashboard header (overall metrics + boot time).
Detailed hardware inventory — PCI devices, GPUs, sensors map.
Live values for sensors that change second to second.
Time series of CPU / package temperatures.
NVIDIA / Intel / AMD GPU live metrics by PCI slot.

health.heading: Health Monitor

rows.use: Small health probe — returns JSON with status, timestamp, version. Suitable for Uptime Kuma keyword checks; the receiver must send the bearer header.
Overall health verdict — single severity + summary string.
All ten categories with per-category statuses and the structured payload that produced each.
Full snapshot — categories + active errors + dismissed list + custom suppression settings. Backs the modal in one round-trip; uses a 6-min background cache for instant response.
Active list, filterable by ?category=<name>.
Dismissed list with remaining suppression hours.
Per-category Suppression Duration values currently configured.
Inventory of Proxmox-defined remote storages, with online state.
Inventory of network interfaces with type (bridge / bond / physical), IP and link speed.
Body: '{'"error_key":"smart_sdh"'}'. Dismiss an alert with the category's configured Suppression Duration.
Manual cleanup of errors whose underlying device or VM is gone. Idempotent.

health.outro: Response shapes and the semantics of the categories live in <link>Health Monitor</link>.

rows.use: All disks visible to the host (block devices, ZFS pools, LVM).
Compact summary used by dashboard cards.
Proxmox-defined storages from /etc/pve/storage.cfg with online state and free space.
Permanent disk observation history — SMART warnings, I/O errors, ZFS pool events, kept across error auto-resolves.
Current SMART attributes for one disk.
Most recent SMART self-test for the disk.
List of stored SMART reports for the disk.
Read a specific stored SMART report.
Trigger a SMART self-test. Body: '{'"type":"short"|"long"'}'.
List the currently scheduled SMART tests.
Detect whether smartctl and friends are installed.
All interfaces with link state and addresses.
Compact view used by the dashboard.
Per-interface RX / TX, error counters, RRD time series.
Latest gateway latency probe.
Time series of gateway latency.
List of all VMs and CTs with status, vmid, name.
Full detail for one guest (config, network, disks).
CPU / memory / disk I/O time series for one guest (RRD).
Recent task logs scoped to that guest.
List backups currently held for this guest.
Body: '{'"action":"start|stop|reboot|shutdown"'}'. Power-cycle a guest.
Trigger vzdump for that guest. Body chooses storage and mode (snapshot / suspend / stop).
Update the description (notes) field of a VM / CT. Other config keys are not modifiable from this endpoint.
Aggregated node-level metrics (RRD).
Cluster-wide list of backups.
Storages flagged as backup targets, with free space.
Filtered journal entries. Query: ?level=&service=&limit=.
Plain-text dump of the filtered range.
Internal event stream — the same one that feeds notifications.
Plain-text complete log for one Proxmox task by UPID.

notifications.heading: Notifications & AI
notifications.intro: The dispatch pipeline, channel walk-throughs and AI rewriter setup live in <notifLink>Notifications</notifLink> and <aiLink>AI Assistant</aiLink>.

rows.use: Recent notifications surfaced in the dashboard.
Export notifications as text.
Dispatcher status — whether the background thread is running, queue depth, last send.
Read the full notification config (channels, per-event toggles, AI rewriter, Display Name).
Dispatch history. Query: ?limit=&offset=&severity=&channel=.
Wipe the dispatch history table.
Send a test notification to one channel. Body: '{'"channel":"telegram"'}'.
Emit a custom event. Body: '{'"event_type":"custom","severity":"WARNING","title":"...","body":"...","data":'{''}''}'.
Test the AI provider connection. Body: '{'"provider","api_key","model","ollama_url?"'}'.
List available models for the selected AI provider.
Register the Monitor as a webhook target in /etc/pve/notifications.cfg.
Remove the Monitor target from PVE's notification config.
Read PVE's current notifications.cfg as PVE sees it.
PVE firewall state and active rules.
Fail2Ban jail status — only useful when the optional jail is installed.
Recent Fail2Ban events (bans, unbans, jail starts).
Lynis last-run status (whether installed, last scan timestamp).
Latest Lynis audit report (warnings, suggestions, hardening index).
Detect which optional security tools (Fail2Ban, Lynis) are installed.
Whether ProxMenux Monitor has an update available, current and latest version.
List of every ProxMenux post-install optimization currently registered on the host (from /usr/local/share/proxmenux/installed_tools.json).
Source code of a specific post-install function — the exact bash that was applied.

prometheus.heading: Prometheus metrics
prometheus.intro: ProxMenux Monitor exposes a Prometheus-format scrape endpoint at GET /api/prometheus (authenticated) returning OpenMetrics-format text. Every metric is labelled with node="<hostname>" and carries an explicit timestamp so it ingests cleanly into Prometheus, VictoriaMetrics, Mimir or any compatible TSDB.

metrics.desc: Total disk space per mount. Label mountpoint.
Total number of VMs and LXCs.
Per-VM/CT memory used and configured maximum.

prometheus.scrapeIntro: The endpoint requires authentication. Pass the API token as a bearer header in your Prometheus scrape config:
prometheus.perHostBody: Each ProxMenux Monitor instance exports metrics for the host it runs on. In a cluster, point Prometheus at every node — the node label on every series lets you distinguish them in Grafana queries (proxmox_vms_running'{'node="pve01"'}').

puttingItTogether.heading: Putting it together
puttingItTogether.body: For end-to-end recipes wiring these endpoints into Home Assistant sensors, Homepage cards, Grafana dashboards, n8n flows and other tools, see the dedicated <link>Integrations</link> page — it walks through the typical setup for each platform with copy-paste configuration. This page stays focused on the catalogue itself.
